title: Roasted Crisp Chinese Black Olives Kung Pao
description: A vegetarian-friendly version of the classic Kung Pao dish, made with roasted and crisp black olives. This dish is a perfect example of Sichuan cuisine's bold flavors and techniques.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 1 tablespoon of rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon of cornstarch, and 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.
In a separate bowl, toss the black olives with 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper.
In a wok or large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the Sichuan peppercorns and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the black olives and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, until they start to crisp up.
Pour in the soy sauce mixture and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes, until the olives are coated and crispy.
Transfer the olives to a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 10-12 minutes, until crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
Serve immediately, garnished with sesame seeds and scallions if desired.
